vBRIEFInfo.description: Scope vBRIEF for #481: add patterns/ directory and patterns/llm-app.md (LLM application standards). Structural unblocker for the patterns/ category.

plan.title: feat(patterns): add patterns/ directory and patterns/llm-app.md for LLM application standards

narratives.Description: Create a new top-level patterns/ directory for cross-language architectural patterns (how to architect this *kind* of system, orthogonal to languages/, interfaces/, platforms/, coding/). First file: patterns/llm-app.md covering prompt construction (delimiters, parameterized templates), explicit trust tiers (system > few-shot > user > retrieved > web), tool/function-call validation (confused-deputy mitigation), RAG hygiene (no LLM-write-back, provenance), output handling (schema validation, XSS sanitisation), multi-agent orchestration (sub-agent-output-is-untrusted), and LLM-specific observability. Add REFERENCES.md lazy-load trigger and a short coding/coding.md addendum. Source material: AI Agent Traps paper (docs/ssrn-6372438.pdf).
narratives.Acceptance: patterns/ directory exists with patterns/llm-app.md authored per #481 body; REFERENCES.md gains a lazy-load trigger for patterns/llm-app.md when project uses LLM APIs; coding/coding.md carries a short addendum cross-linking patterns/llm-app.md; tools/telemetry.md gains an LLM-specific observability section.

x-migrator.PhaseDescription: Near-up structural priority -- post-cutover stabilization, structural unblockers (items gating many others), and cross-cutting items whose effect spans multiple skills, strategies, or files.
x-migrator.Description: Add patterns/ directory + patterns/llm-app.md (#481). Structural unblocker for ~22 dependent open issues with the patterns label.
x-migrator.Rationale: Promoted to Phase 2 (rather than Phase 3) because 22 open issues in the patterns/ category are blocked on this; placement in Phase 2 reflects structural priority rather than the kind of work.

references.title: Issue #481: feat(patterns): add patterns/ directory and patterns/llm-app.md for LLM application standards
Companion: #482 (move coding/holzmann.md -> patterns/safety-critical.md and swarm/swarm.md -> patterns/multi-agent.md)
